layman_headline: Extreme fear grips crypto as geopolitical tensions and inflation fears keep traders on the sidelines.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode right now, with a score of just 22 out of 100. Bitcoin is holding around $62,600 but faces major headwinds: the Iran/Hormuz conflict is flaring up again, a U.S. CPI inflation report is imminent, and spot Bitcoin ETFs just saw $425 million in outflows. The altcoins on our watchlist are all in downtrends with poor chart structures — none of them offer a clean enough setup to risk capital on right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is under heavy pressure from geopolitics and macro uncertainty. When fear is this extreme, bounces can be violent — but so can further drops. Wait for a confirmed reversal signal or a clear catalyst before committing money.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate fails critical filters. GRASS is down 32% in 7 days but has a risk/reward of only 0.69x with no support confirmation — a falling knife. KITE has an attractive 2.38x R/R and a bullish engulfing candle, but is in a hard downtrend with -20% 5-day momentum and no volume data to confirm the reversal. JTO has the best chart bias (+0.40) but RSI at 59 is already elevated after a recent run, and the doji signals indecision — not conviction. MON's bullish marubozu is interesting but R/R at 0.79x is insufficient. XPL is deeply bearish (-0.70 bias). The macro backdrop is hostile: $425M ETF outflows, Iran conflict escalation with an 8-hour deadline per CryptoSlate, pending CPI data, and the U.S. government just moved $288M in seized crypto to Coinbase Prime (potential sell pressure). Our signal credibility data shows volume_breakout signals have 100% win rate — none of these candidates show that signal. Discipline demands we wait.
risk_factors: Iran/Hormuz conflict escalation could trigger a broad risk-off crash, sending BTC below $60K and alts down 20%+
CPI report could come in hot, boosting July rate hike bets and crushing crypto further
U.S. government's $288M crypto transfer to Coinbase Prime could signal imminent selling pressure
watch_list: CPI inflation report release — hot print could send BTC below $60K support
Iran/Hormuz conflict developments in next 8-24 hours — de-escalation would be bullish
Bitcoin ETF flow data — need to see outflow reversal before alts can recover
market_context: Crypto is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with heavy macro headwinds. BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital fleeing alts to Bitcoin, and even Bitcoin is struggling to hold $62.6K amid geopolitical risk and upcoming inflation data.
context_banner: Multiple macro risks converging — geopolitical conflict, CPI uncertainty, and ETF outflows demand patience over action.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 suggests market is near capitulation, but improving trend (from 23.9 weekly avg) is not yet confirmed — too early to buy the fear.
US spot Bitcoin ETFs posted $425M outflow after a brief rebound, indicating institutional selling pressure is resuming — bearish for near-term price action.
Bitcoin has less than 8 hours to hold $60K before Hormuz conflict escalation takes over — imminent binary event risk.
U.S. government moved $288M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime, signaling potential government selling that could add supply pressure.
No swing candidate has RSI below 35 (true oversold). MON at 37 and XPL at 39 are approaching but not yet at levels that historically produce reliable bounces.
